# Questions in Livestream 113

Live Stream #113: Questioning the Lore

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=449" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:29</a> | What should viewers make of the claim that the World Economic Forum called for an AI-written Bible? | He says there is no evidence the forum as a body called for that, even though AI tools can obviously generate text on demand. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:27</a> | Why did a Twelfth Dynasty ruler use a name tied to Sobek, and was Hapy or even vegetables worshiped? | He ties the Sobek connection to the Fayum and the dynasty's move toward that region; Hapy was worshiped, but Egyptians did not generally worship vegetables. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=887" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:47</a> | What does Dr. Falk think of Finkelstein's and Sager's early Iron Age town population-density estimates? | He says archaeological demography is imprecise and densities vary; Egyptian settlements could be much denser than early Iron Age Levantine towns, so population estimates should be treated as ranges. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=1049" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:29</a> | How does the clan interpretation of Genesis 5 make sense of Enoch? | He treats Enoch within the clan structure and says the notice points toward a tradition of a group walking with God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=1347" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:27</a> | What is Dr. Falk's favorite prophecy and why? | He picks Isaiah 53 because he sees it as a suffering-servant passage that ties many Old Testament themes together in a messianic vision. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=1690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:10</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on Young's Literal Translation? | He calls it historically interesting and very literal, but too wooden for serious apologetics or exegetical work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=1959" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:39</a> | Does John 1:49 relate to Gnostic ideas of sparks becoming suns, or only to Stoicism's claim that Jesus was cosmic order itself? | He says the Logos language in John is older and broader than Gnosticism, drawing on Hellenistic Jewish and philosophical vocabulary rather than a simple Gnostic parallel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=2233" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:13</a> | What is going on with the rods in Genesis 30:35-41; is it sorcery? | He argues Jacob is not doing magic but using bark with medicinal properties to strengthen the flock. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=2714" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:14</a> | How can Jacob fight God in Genesis 32:22-30 and somehow win? | He treats the scene as a divine encounter tied to Jacob's transformation and name change rather than a simple physical victory over God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=3117" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:57</a> | Is there a relationship between exorcising a demon by knowing its name and Egyptian ideas about knowing a god's true name? | He says yes; in the ancient Near East knowing a true name was associated with special power or access, though Yahweh cannot be manipulated that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=3387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:27</a> | If Samuel's appearance to Saul was not a ghost, what exactly is a ghost? | He clarifies that Samuel could be called a ghost, spirit, or apparition, but "ghost" is a broader term than the narrower apparition language he prefers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=3549" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:09</a> | Does Dr. Falk have a favorite version of the Book of Common Prayer? | He does not use it much, but says he would probably choose the clearer 2019 version over the 1662 language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=3630" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:30</a> | What best explains the similarity between Ezekiel's four living beings and Mesopotamian apkallu or cherub imagery? | He says Ezekiel's imagery is much closer to Mesopotamian cherubim than apkallu and reflects later Mesopotamian influence on Israelite literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=4076" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:56</a> | Why is Graham Hancock so popular when his ideas are not new? | He attributes the popularity to strong storytelling, saying fringe ideas gain traction when presented by engaging writers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=4259" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:59</a> | What does Dr. Falk make of Michael Heiser's view that accreditation for the Awakening School was unnecessary? | He agrees accreditation is unnecessary for certificates of completion, but says the school's marketing about "doctoral level" courses is misleading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=4587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:27</a> | Did Dr. Falk ever wish he lived in 1910-1920 so he could meet future kings and tribal leaders during fieldwork? | He says no, because those years include World War I; he would rather avoid that decade even if surrounding years were strong for archaeology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=4726" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:46</a> | Is there literature explaining the beliefs of Continental young earth creationists? | He says they tend to produce tracts and small booklets through anti-Darwin societies rather than large American-style creationist books. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=4938" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:18</a> | Who are Dr. Falk's top three favorite church fathers, and why? | Off the top of his head he names Irenaeus, Athanasius, and Chrysostom, while noting the field is vast. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=5029" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:49</a> | Has Dr. Falk read Mark S. Smith, and does he have anything to say about him? | He says Smith is an excellent scholar, though he disagrees with Smith's view of early Israelite polytheism and warns against quote-mining him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=5245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:25</a> | Why does Dr. Falk refer to early pharaohs as kings, and is that common in Egyptology? | He says it is common because "pharaoh" as a title begins later, so earlier rulers are often called kings even though broad "pharaonic" language is also used. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=5574" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:54</a> | Has Dr. Falk seen the new Indiana Jones movie? | He says he has not seen it and is wary because later Indiana Jones films disappointed him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=5666" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:26</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on Dr. Michael Brown? | He says the lectures he has heard on slavery in the ancient Near East were quite good, though he does not know Brown's full background. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=5745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:45</a> | Can Dr. Falk elaborate on his eschatological position given his Old Testament background? | He downplays eschatology compared with orthodoxy and orthopraxy, then identifies himself as post-tribulational and historic premillennial. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=6100" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:40</a> | What is the best approach when speaking to atheists? | He advises a friendly, genuine, sensitive approach that accounts for the person's background rather than rushing the exchange. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=6545" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:05</a> | Can Dr. Falk speak ancient Egyptian? | He says he can read and pronounce ancient Egyptian, but speaking a language is a separate skill and not the main scholarly need. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=6641" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:41</a> | What does Exodus 4:24 mean when the Lord sought to put Moses to death? | He says God was going to kill Moses because Moses had failed to circumcise his son before serving as covenant representative for Israel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=6857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:17</a> | Can we know how ancient Egyptians sounded? | He says we can know late Egyptian reasonably well through Coptic, but old and middle Egyptian pronunciation is less certain because of vowel and consonant shifts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=7050" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:30</a> | What language are they speaking, or trying to speak, in Stargate SG-1? | He says it is a very butchered fictional development of Egyptian, not true Egyptian, though it appears to assume the language kept changing over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=7181" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:41</a> | Were there any sounds Egyptians could not pronounce, and did they have an accent? | He says everyone has an accent, and Egyptians seem to have had difficulty distinguishing r and l sounds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=7539" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:39</a> | Was Moses' adoption by Egyptians an unusual practice? | He says no; adoption and fosterage arrangements were common, and both men and women could adopt in Egyptian society. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=7778" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:38</a> | What is Dr. Falk's favorite elevator argument for God's existence? | He says he does not really use arguments for God's existence and instead prefers a softer, pragmatic approach focused on the Bible's historicity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=7949" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:29</a> | Was Ahaziah 22 the first time he ruled and 42 the second time, and how does that square with a one-year reign? | He checks the passages and says the issue appears textual; modern translations follow the reading that gives 22. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=8433" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:33</a> | What should Christians know about the transmission of Scripture and how we get the modern Bible? | He emphasizes that English Bibles result from textual scholarship and translation choices, so translations are interpretations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=8702" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:02</a> | Are the Septuagint numbers the most accurate? | He says Old Testament text criticism must be handled case by case; sometimes Septuagint numbers are better, sometimes not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=9163" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:43</a> | What is the meaning of Acts 8:14-17, since it does not seem to support common Protestant or Catholic views of salvation? | He treats the passage as a special circumstance tied to Samaria, prophecy fulfillment, and showing that the Holy Spirit cannot be manipulated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=9836" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:56</a> | When Egyptians put Anubis to bed, was he dog tired, and would the same question about Seth be more controversial? | He answers through the Anubis shrine cloak from Tutankhamun's tomb and says too little survives from Seth's known temple to know its practice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=10213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:13</a> | If Dr. Falk could make his own bumper sticker in any language, what would it say? | He would write it in hieroglyphs: "If you can read this, you're overeducated." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=10299" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:39</a> | When Yeshua died, did he ascend to heaven with the thief or descend to Sheol to preach to the souls? | He says the timing is complex, but Paradise language can include the blessed dead while Christ's own death precedes the thieves' deaths. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=10544" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:44</a> | Why are only Job's daughters named? | He says the names likely point to Job's restored abundance and blessing, but notes he would need more study for the first name's significance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=10960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:40</a> | Are there good Eastern European Egyptologists or Bible scholars writing on early Exodus questions? | He knows good Eastern European Egyptologists, especially from the Czech Republic, but not good Bible scholars writing in that specific early Exodus area. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=11371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:31</a> | Do all Christians find the date of the Exodus touchy, or does that depend on church tradition? | He says it falls along denominational lines; mainstream Protestants, Catholics, and Orthodox often do not care, while evangelical and fundamentalist circles tend to be touchier. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=11697" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:57</a> | What part did the Bronze Age collapse have in the decline and fall of the New Kingdom? | He says it affected Egypt through drought and regional disruption, reaching Egypt later and contributing to the end of Dynasty 20 and related economic stress. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=12003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:03</a> | Did Egyptians stop hyperinflation by increasing supply? | He says that in a barter economy, increasing the supply of goods from satellite territories was how inflation could be reduced. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=12048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:48</a> | What extra-biblical evidence is there for Joseph's land policy in Genesis 47? | He points to Egyptian land and tax arrangements, including priestly exemptions, as meaningful parallels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=12379" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:19</a> | Did Joseph's land and tax policy get upended later? | He says it was changed but not fully redressed; the Dynasty 18 regime broke up land grants and distributed them, while taxation continued through the new landholders. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=12522" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:42</a> | What were Amenhotep III's religious reforms, and why were they important? | He says the reforms reduced the number of deities at important cult sites and helped move the king toward greater solarization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=12762" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:42</a> | Which English Bible translation is most accurate for study purposes? | He especially commends the NASB 1977 for scholarly study and speaks favorably of the CSB as a strong modern study option. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=12859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:19</a> | What are Dr. Falk's thoughts on the NET Bible? | He says he has heard good things but has not read or evaluated it himself, so he cannot recommend it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/KSYXqKQnJxA?t=13949" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:29</a> | Has Dr. Falk ever played Shin Megami Tensei? | He says no; he has not had much time for video games recently. |
